title: Scan my project - SonarQube for IDE Documentation - IntelliJ
content:
# Scan my project ## [Language-specific information](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#language-specific-information "Language-specific information") SonarQube for IDE in Rider The Sonar C# analyzer is written as a Roslyn analyzer. We leverage the [OmniSharp](https://github.com/OmniSharp/omnisharp-roslyn "OmniSharp") project (the language server used by VSCode) as a "runner" for our analyzer. ## [Supported features in Rider](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#supported-features-in-rider "Supported features in Rider") - Loading of C# projects type supported by VSCode C# (.NET Core, .NET Framework, Unity, ...) (but manual configuration may be required, see `sonar.cs.internal.useNet6` below) - Analyze on the fly. - Analyze the entire solution (may be slow, as files are analyzed one at a time). - Display of rules descriptions. - Setting up Connected Mode. ## [Missing features, known limitations](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#missing-features-known-limitations "Missing features, known limitations") - We can't automatically determine the OmniSharp flavor to use for your project. You have to manually set `sonar.cs.internal.useNet6` to `false` for legacy or Mono projects. - No VB.NET support (let us know if you are interested). - Big solutions can take a lot of time to load/consume a lot of resources. See the Advanced Configuration section below for a list of properties to configure. - If you have a `~/.omnisharp/omnisharp.json` configuration file, it will conflict with SonarQube for IDE. Please remove it. - Solutions mixing "new" and "legacy" projects are partially supported. Depending on the value of `sonar.cs.internal.useNet6`, only the supported projects will be loaded and have issues reported. ## [Advanced configuration](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#advanced-configuration "Advanced configuration") We aim at making SonarQube for IDE easy to use, and smart enough to avoid manual configuration. Unfortunately, we are not there yet for Rider. So here are a few properties you can set to tune embedded OmniSharp behavior:
Analysis PropertyDefault valueDescription
sonar.cs.internal.useNet6trueUse the modern Net 6 build of OmniSharp. If you are working with a Mono solution on Linux/Unix, or with an old NET Framework solution on Windows (< 4), set it to false
sonar.cs.internal.loadProjectsOnDemandfalseBy default the all projects of the solution will be loaded. This ensure maximum rules accuracy. For very big solutions that take too long to load, change this to true.
sonar.cs.internal.startupTimeout60Maximum waiting time (in seconds) for OmniSharp to start
sonar.cs.internal.loadProjectsTimeout60Maximum waiting time (in seconds) for OmniSharp to load all projects (not relevant if sonar.cs.internal.loadProjectsOnDemand is set to true)
To define those properties, go to **SonarQube for IDE Project Settings**: ![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed) ![Go to "Configure SonarQube for IDE" > "Analysis Properties" to set your property configurations manually.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/f9260f15-59a3-4af0-bb64-abe481b3104c/sq-intellij-set-analysis-property.png?w=2546&h=1522&auto=format&fit=crop "Go to "Configure SonarQube for IDE" > "Analysis Properties" to set your property configurations manually.") ## [Reporting issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#reporting-issues "Reporting issues") We are interested in your [feedback](https://community.sonarsource.com/c/sl/fault/6 "feedback"). In case of an issue, please share: - Your version of Rider: - Your project/solution target and type (.NET Core, .NET Framework, ASP, Unity, ...): - The version of SonarQube for IDE you installed: - SonarQube for IDE + OmniSharp logs: in the SonarQube for IDE console, you have to [enable verbose/analysis logs](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/troubleshooting/ "enable verbose/analysis logs"). ## [Supported features in Rider](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#supported-features-in-rider "Supported features in Rider") - Loading of C# projects type supported by VSCode C# (.NET Core, .NET Framework, Unity, ...) (but manual configuration may be required, see `sonar.cs.internal.useNet6` below) - Analyze on the fly. - Analyze the entire solution (may be slow, as files are analyzed one at a time). - Display of rules descriptions. - Setting up Connected Mode. ## [Missing features, known limitations](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#missing-features-known-limitations "Missing features, known limitations") - We can't automatically determine the OmniSharp flavor to use for your project. You have to manually set `sonar.cs.internal.useNet6` to `false` for legacy or Mono projects. - No VB.NET support (let us know if you are interested). - Big solutions can take a lot of time to load/consume a lot of resources. See the Advanced Configuration section below for a list of properties to configure. - If you have a `~/.omnisharp/omnisharp.json` configuration file, it will conflict with SonarQube for IDE. Please remove it. - Solutions mixing "new" and "legacy" projects are partially supported. Depending on the value of `sonar.cs.internal.useNet6`, only the supported projects will be loaded and have issues reported. ## [Advanced configuration](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#advanced-configuration "Advanced configuration") We aim at making SonarQube for IDE easy to use, and smart enough to avoid manual configuration. Unfortunately, we are not there yet for Rider. So here are a few properties you can set to tune embedded OmniSharp behavior:
Analysis PropertyDefault valueDescription
sonar.cs.internal.useNet6trueUse the modern Net 6 build of OmniSharp. If you are working with a Mono solution on Linux/Unix, or with an old NET Framework solution on Windows (< 4), set it to false
sonar.cs.internal.loadProjectsOnDemandfalseBy default the all projects of the solution will be loaded. This ensure maximum rules accuracy. For very big solutions that take too long to load, change this to true.
sonar.cs.internal.startupTimeout60Maximum waiting time (in seconds) for OmniSharp to start
sonar.cs.internal.loadProjectsTimeout60Maximum waiting time (in seconds) for OmniSharp to load all projects (not relevant if sonar.cs.internal.loadProjectsOnDemand is set to true)
To define those properties, go to **SonarQube for IDE Project Settings**: ![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed) ![Go to "Configure SonarQube for IDE" > "Analysis Properties" to set your property configurations manually.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/f9260f15-59a3-4af0-bb64-abe481b3104c/sq-intellij-set-analysis-property.png?w=2546&h=1522&auto=format&fit=crop "Go to "Configure SonarQube for IDE" > "Analysis Properties" to set your property configurations manually.") ## [Reporting issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/scan-my-project/#reporting-issues "Reporting issues") We are interested in your [feedback](https://community.sonarsource.com/c/sl/fault/6 "feedback"). In case of an issue, please share: - Your version of Rider: - Your project/solution target and type (.NET Core, .NET Framework, ASP, Unity, ...): - The version of SonarQube for IDE you installed: - SonarQube for IDE + OmniSharp logs: in the SonarQube for IDE console, you have to [enable verbose/analysis logs](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/troubleshooting/ "enable verbose/analysis logs").
